compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
ROOT CAUSE (proven, joe-debug protocol). Asymmetry between the two differs: the OUTBOUND differ (outbound_fields.py:436-437) applies fit_text_to_adf_limit(local_val) before comparing so it converges once Jira holds the send-side-truncated body; the INBOUND differ (_diff_jira_vs_local) applied only rstrip() tolerance, so when Jira holds the truncated description it saw local(full) != jira(truncated) and emitted changed['description']=<truncated>, which apply_inbound._apply_inbound_update wrote as a local EDIT — clobbering the full description and invalidating the plan-review fingerprint. Crucially, outbound convergence REMOVES the only cross-direction guard (bidir suppression) that could have masked this, so it reproduces at steady state.

PROOF: RED unit test emitted a mutation containing the ' … [truncated by reconciler]' suffix; the genuine-edit case stayed GREEN (no over-suppression). Fix held out from the fixer subagent; GREEN against the held-out oracle; mutation check (disable the fit) returned it to RED — the test has teeth. New integration round-trip test (real outbound+inbound differs, full rebar->Jira-ADF-truncate->rebar chain) is RED without the fix, GREEN with it, and asserts the local ticket keeps its full description.

FIX: inbound_differ._diff_jira_vs_local now applies _load_adf().fit_text_to_adf_limit(local_val) to the local description before comparison (in-memory copy only; never written into changed) — the inbound mirror of the outbound convergence guard. 331 reconciler tests pass; ruff clean.

compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Local ticket has a description long enough that its ADF representation exceeds Jira's ~32k limit (`_ADF_DESCRIPTION_LIMIT = 32000`).
2. Outbound sync truncates the description via `adf.fit_text_to_adf_limit` so it fits Jira (send-side only, by design).
3. Jira now stores the TRUNCATED description.
4. On the next inbound pass, `inbound_differ._diff_jira_vs_local` compares Jira's truncated description against local's full description.

**Expected:** Inbound recognises Jira's body as the truncated form of local and emits NO description change; the local description (and its plan-review fingerprint/signature) is untouched.

**Actual:** Inbound only `rstrip()`-compares, sees the truncated Jira body != full local body, and emits `changed["description"] = <truncated>`. The applier writes the truncated body into the local store, clobbering the full description and invalidating the plan-review attestation.

## Root Cause
Asymmetry between the two differs:
- Outbound (`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_fields.py:436-437`) applies `fit_text_to_adf_limit(local_val)` before comparing, so it converges.
- Inbound (`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_differ.py` `_diff_jira_vs_local`, ~L295-302) does NOT apply the same transform to the local value before comparison.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Inbound differ applies the identical `fit_text_to_adf_limit` transform to the local description before comparing to Jira, mirroring the outbound convergence fix.
- [ ] When Jira holds only the truncated form of an otherwise-identical local description, NO inbound description mutation is emitted (local store + fingerprint unchanged).
- [ ] A genuine Jira-side description edit (not merely truncation) STILL flows inbound (no regression to inbound field-sync).
- [ ] Unit test (RED→GREEN) in tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing covering the truncation-equal case and the genuine-edit case.
- [ ] E2E/integration test covering the full outbound-truncate → Jira-holds-truncated → inbound-pass round-trip asserting local description is unchanged.
compiled_state.title: Inbound differ clobbers local description with Jira-truncated body, invalidating plan-review signature

verify_commands.dd_text: Inbound differ suppresses Jira-truncated description; genuine edit still flows
Full outbound-truncate to inbound round-trip leaves local description unchanged
